# Psh command compatibility

Specification schema: `1.0`. Psh version: `0.1.0`.

Core uses PowerShell implementations and contains no third-party utility executables. Full delegates `rg`, `fd`, `jq`, and `bat` to pinned native tools; all other commands keep the PowerShell backend.

For the four delegated commands, the flags listed below are common completion hints; Full accepts each pinned native tool's complete argument set.

## Editions

| Edition | Native tools | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Core | - | PowerShell implementations only; no third-party utility executables. |
| Full | rg, fd, jq, bat | Core plus pinned native ripgrep, fd, jq, and bat executables. |

## Exit codes

| Code | Name | Meaning |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 0 | Success | The command completed successfully. |
| 1 | NoMatch | The command completed but found no matching result. |
| 2 | UsageError | The arguments or requested syntax are unsupported or invalid. |
| 3 | RuntimeError | The command failed while performing the requested operation. |
| 4 | MissingDependency | A required dependency or active backend is unavailable. |
| 5 | IntegrityFailure | A package, dependency, or generated artifact failed integrity validation. |

## Object APIs

| API | Commands | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Find-PshText` | grep, rg | Returns structured text-search matches. |
| `Find-PshItem` | find, fd | Returns structured file-system search results. |
| `Select-PshJson` | jq | Returns objects selected by the Core jq expression subset. |
| `Get-PshHead` | head | Returns leading records or bytes without text formatting. |
| `Get-PshTail` | tail | Returns trailing records or bytes without text formatting. |
| `Measure-PshText` | wc | Returns structured line, word, character, and byte counts. |
| `Set-PshFileTime` | touch | Creates files or updates their access and modification times. |
| `Invoke-PshXArgs` | xargs | Invokes commands with argument arrays and without expression evaluation. |

## Management commands

| Action | JSON | Flags | Description | Example |
| --- | :---: | --- | --- | --- |
| `psh version` | no | `--help` | Show the installed Psh version and edition. | `psh version` |
| `psh doctor` | yes | `--json` `--help` | Validate installation, configuration, profiles, and active backends. | `psh doctor` |
| `psh capabilities` | yes | `--json` `--help` | List commands, object APIs, editions, and active backends. | `psh capabilities --json` |
| `psh commands` | no | `--category` `--name` `--help` | List compatible command names and their categories. | `psh commands` |
| `psh config` | no | `get` `set` `reset` `--help` | Read, set, or reset current-user Psh configuration. | `psh config get` |
| `psh update` | no | `--version` `--edition` `--non-interactive` `--help` | Install and atomically activate a verified Psh version. | `psh update` |
| `psh rollback` | no | `--version` `--non-interactive` `--help` | Atomically reactivate a retained previous Psh version. | `psh rollback` |
| `psh self-test` | no | `--quick` `--help` | Run local command and installation health checks. | `psh self-test` |
| `psh uninstall` | no | `--keep-config` `--non-interactive` `--help` | Remove Psh-owned files and restore backed-up profile content. | `psh uninstall` |
